Setup in 3 steps

From download to running in 60 seconds

1
Install Claude Code
One npm command. Requires Node.js 18+. Works on Mac, Linux, and Windows.
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
2
Copy the skills folder
Drop the skills into your Claude Code directory. No config files, no setup wizard.
cp -r skills/ ~/.claude/skills/
3
Invoke any skill
Open any project, run claude, and call skills by name. That's it.
> /code-review
> /write-tests
> /debug-smart

FAQ

Questions

Claude Code is Anthropic's official CLI tool — an AI assistant that runs in your terminal and can read your files, write code, run commands, and edit your project. Unlike browser-based AI, it actually operates inside your codebase.
You need basic terminal comfort (opening Terminal, running a command). The skills themselves are usable by anyone with a codebase. Many of the reference skills work as teaching tools — you can ask Claude to explain what it found.
All skills are language-agnostic unless noted. Code examples use TypeScript/JavaScript, Python, and SQL where relevant. The principles apply to any stack — React, Node.js, Python, Ruby, Go, whatever you're building in.
No — this is a third-party bundle made by Focus AI. The skills use Anthropic's official SKILL.md format (documented in the Claude Code docs), but the content was written by us based on software engineering best practices.
Claude Code uses the Anthropic API separately from Claude.ai. You'll need an API key from console.anthropic.com with API credits. New accounts get free credits to get started. Typical usage runs $2–10/month.
Absolutely. Each skill is a plain Markdown file you can edit freely. The README includes the full SKILL.md format spec so you can create your own. Many developers customize the existing skills for their team's conventions.

Make Claude Code actually useful.

50 skills. One command to install. Yours forever.
